description_long: This is a small, pale-colored crab with a broad, rounded cephalothorax and a pair of prominent, asymmetrical claws. Its exoskeleton is mostly off-white to light brown, with subtle darker markings on the dorsal surface and legs. The crab has eight slender walking legs, each jointed and slightly curved, radiating from the body. Its eyes are set on short stalks, positioned at the front of the cephalothorax, and two short antennae are visible. The claws are robust, with one noticeably larger than the other, and have a smooth, slightly glossy finish. The overall appearance suggests a lightweight, hard-shelled crustacean commonly found in coastal or urban environments.
description: A small, pale crab with a rounded body, eight slender legs, and two prominent claws, one larger than the other. Its exoskeleton is hard and lightly patterned, with eyes on short stalks.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the broad cephalothorax, both claws raised, and all eight legs splayed outward; eyes and antennae are clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the asymmetry of the claws, the curvature of the legs, and the profile of the body; one claw appears much larger.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the darker dorsal markings, the arrangement of the legs, and the back of the cephalothorax; claws are visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the smaller claw in the foreground, the segmentation of the legs, and the side profile of the eyes and antennae.
